description: Fix the plugin scaffolder to generate packages compatible with third-party npm publishing. The scaffolder currently generates @opentabs-dev-scoped package names and restricted publishConfig, making it impossible for third-party developers to publish plugins to npm.

userStories.title: Generate unscoped package names and remove restrictive publishConfig in scaffolder
userStories.description: As a third-party plugin developer, I want the scaffolder to generate a publishable package.json so that I can npm publish my plugin without manually editing the generated metadata.
acceptanceCriteria: Scaffolded package.json does NOT contain `publishConfig: { access: 'restricted' }` — either omit publishConfig entirely or set `access: 'public'`
Scaffolded package.json `version` field is `0.1.0`, not the SDK version
Console log during scaffolding says 'Creating opentabs-plugin-<name>' (unscoped), not '@opentabs-dev/opentabs-plugin-<name>'
README.md template package name remains consistent with the actual package.json name
Existing tests in platform/cli/src/scaffold.test.ts pass (update assertions to match new unscoped name)
userStories.notes:
The fix is in platform/cli/src/scaffold.ts.

Line 143: Change `name: \`@opentabs-dev/opentabs-plugin-${args.name}\`` to `name: \`opentabs-plugin-${args.name}\``

Line 145: Change `version: openTabsVersion.replace(/^\^/, '')` to `version: '0.1.0'`

Line 146: Remove `publishConfig: { access: 'restricted' }` entirely (unscoped packages are public by default).

Line 604: Change the console.log to match: `Creating ${pc.bold(\`opentabs-plugin-${args.name}\`)}...`

Line 306 in the README template already uses the unscoped name (`opentabs-plugin-${args.name}`) — verify it stays consistent.

IMPORTANT: The @opentabs-dev scope is ONLY for official platform packages published by the opentabs team. Third-party developers use unscoped package names like `opentabs-plugin-slack`. The auto-discovery in platform/mcp-server/src/discovery.ts already handles both scoped and unscoped names.

Also check platform/cli/src/scaffold.test.ts — update any assertions that expect the old scoped package name to expect the new unscoped name. Similarly update any version assertions that compare against the SDK version to expect '0.1.0'.
